title: Tell Us What Happened
subtitle: A simple guided field report in your own words.
description: A short voice-or-text response path for someone reporting what they saw, when it happened, what changed, what they tried, and what help is needed. No photographs or screenshots are required.

sections.label: What Happened
sections.description: Describe the situation as you experienced it. Ordinary words are best.

sections.label: What You Did and Need
sections.description: Record what changed, what you tried, and what would help now.

prompts.text: Where were you, and what were you looking at or working with? Describe the place or item so someone who is not there can identify it.
prompts.context: Names, labels, room descriptions, or nearby landmarks are useful. Do not include private information that is unrelated to the report.

prompts.text: What did you notice? Tell the story in your own words, including anything you saw, heard, smelled, or felt was unusual.
prompts.context: There is no preferred wording. Describe only what you remember or observed.

prompts.text: When did this begin, and what was happening immediately before you first noticed it?
prompts.context: An approximate day, time, shift, or sequence is enough if you do not know the exact time.

prompts.text: Has it happened once or more than once? Describe any pattern, trigger, delay, or condition that seems connected.
prompts.context: It is fine to say that you do not know or that there is no obvious pattern.

prompts.text: What, if anything, changed before this started? Consider people, settings, equipment, supplies, weather, cleaning, maintenance, or normal routines.
prompts.context: Do not guess. Saying that nothing changed, or that you do not know, is useful.

prompts.text: What have you or someone else already tried, and what happened afterward?
prompts.context: Include temporary improvements, things that made no difference, and anything you stopped for safety.

prompts.text: What is the situation right now? Is anything unsafe, unavailable, intermittent, or preventing normal work?
prompts.context: If anyone may be in immediate danger, stop and contact the appropriate emergency or qualified service rather than continuing this report.

prompts.text: What do you need the person receiving this report to understand or do next? Add anything important that the earlier questions did not cover.
prompts.context: You can name a practical need, a question, a concern, or simply say that you want someone to review the report.
